products.name: 72V 42Ah High Power Battery
products.description: The 72V swap the Light Bee scene actually runs. Potted NMC pouch cells in a stainless case, 28kW peak. Needs an aftermarket controller to use any of it.

products.name: XLB-60 RACE Motor (Air Cooled)
products.description: Race motor for big-power Light Bee builds. Mandatory battery upgrade to run it; EBMX prices through dealers, street is around $1.8k.

products.name: TC500 Controller Kit
products.description: True plug-and-play power controller. The only aftermarket unit that talks to the stock Sur-Ron and Talaria BMS. Up to 20kW peak, tuned from a phone app.

products.name: BAC4000 Power Upgrade Kit
products.description: The classic Sur-Ron/Segway power kit. Doubles to triples stock power with tune options from 5.2kW to 12kW. Bypass tunes work on the stock battery.

products.name: Custom Wheel Set
products.description: US-built custom wheels: aircraft-grade rims, billet hubs, stainless spokes. Priced per wheel, built to order in 4-6 weeks, ships with rotor and sprocket.

products.name: 17in Supermoto Wheel Set
products.description: The street conversion. CNC 7050-T6 rims, bolts to OEM rotors and sprocket, bearings pre-installed. Run real supermoto rubber.

products.name: 3in Rise Handlebar
products.description: 7075 tapered bar in the OEM 31.8mm clamp. 3 inches of rise fixes the cramped stock cockpit for anyone over 5'10".

products.name: Geomax MX53 70/100-19 Off-Road Tire
products.description: Real MX rubber that fits the stock 19in rim. The single cheapest traction upgrade on these bikes. Priced per tire.

products.name: 60V Aura Battery
products.description: Range play, not a power play: 50-75Ah of 60V that drops in on the stock controller. The move for riders who want miles, not wheelies.

products.name: 67.2V 10A Fast Charger
products.description: Cuts a Light Bee charge from all evening to about 3 hours. 10A with a cooling fan, auto 110/220V. For 60V packs only.

products.name: 72V 42Ah Race Battery (Talaria Sting)
products.description: 28kW Talaria race pack that fits under the OEM lid with no cutting. Not compatible with the stock 60V controller; budget for an X-9000 or Torp.

products.name: Low Rise Handlebar for Super73
products.description: 7/8in low-rise bar that drops the sit-up cruiser posture into something you can actually lean on. Cheap, clean, reversible.

fitments.note: Vendor fitment list: "Surron Light Bee, Surron Ultra Bee, Talaria Sting MX3 / MX4 / MX5, Talaria XXX, Talaria XXX PRO..." — https://parts.electriccyclerider.com/products/x-9000-controller. Years capped at 2025: the 2026 Light Bee X is a redesigned 72V factory platform (per https://surron.ae/blogs/news/2026-sur-ron-light-bee-x-vs-2025-the-biggest-factory-upgrade-yet) and no vendor statement covers it.
Vendor fitment list: "Surron Light Bee, Surron Ultra Bee, Talaria Sting MX3 / MX4 / MX5, Talaria XXX..." — https://parts.electriccyclerider.com/products/x-9000-controller
Vendor fitment list explicitly names "Talaria Sting MX3 / MX4 / MX5" — https://parts.electriccyclerider.com/products/x-9000-controller
Vendor fitment list explicitly names "Talaria XXX, Talaria XXX PRO" — https://parts.electriccyclerider.com/products/x-9000-controller

Vendor lists "Talaria Sting MX3 / MX4 / MX5" (https://parts.electriccyclerider.com/products/x-9000-controller); our bike is the MX5 Pro variant, which is not explicitly named. Confirm Pro-variant fitment before listing.

Vendor fitment list: "Segway X160, Segway X260, Surron Light Bee X, Eride pro SR, 79bike Falcon-M, Surron Light bee S" — https://revrides.com/products/ebmx-high-power-batteries-for-talaria-surron. Requires aftermarket controller (EBMX recommends X-9000). Years capped at 2025: 2026 LBX is a new 72V factory platform not covered by this statement.
Vendor fitment list: "Segway X160, Segway X260, Surron Light Bee X..." — https://revrides.com/products/ebmx-high-power-batteries-for-talaria-surron. Requires aftermarket controller.
Vendor fitment list: "Segway X160, Segway X260, Surron Light Bee X..." — https://revrides.com/products/ebmx-high-power-batteries-for-talaria-surron. Requires aftermarket controller.

Manufacturer: "Harness and mount sets are available to use this motor with SurRon Light Bee or Eride Pro" and "Light Bee... require a mandatory battery upgrade to run the XLB-60 RACE Motor" — https://www.ebmx.com/shop/ebmx-xlb-60-race-motor-air-cooled-4513. Years capped at 2025 (2026 LBX = new 72V platform, not covered).

Manufacturer: "FULLY COMPATIBLE WITH SURRON LIGHT BEE / SEGWAY X260, TALARIA MX3/MX4/MX5 AND THE TORP TM25 MOTOR" — https://www.torpmotors.com/tc500-controller-2/. Years capped at 2025 (2026 LBX = redesigned 72V platform, not covered by statement).
Manufacturer: "FULLY COMPATIBLE WITH SURRON LIGHT BEE / SEGWAY X260, TALARIA MX3/MX4/MX5..." — https://www.torpmotors.com/tc500-controller-2/
Manufacturer: "...TALARIA MX3/MX4/MX5..." — https://www.torpmotors.com/tc500-controller-2/. Note: manufacturer states it currently cannot communicate with SCUD-made stock Talaria batteries.
Manufacturer names "TALARIA MX3/MX4/MX5" (https://www.torpmotors.com/tc500-controller-2/); our bike is the MX5 Pro variant, not explicitly named. Confirm Pro-variant fitment.

Vendor tune chart: "5.2kW 48V Bypass Tune = Use on the Segway X160 or SurRon Youth 48V with Bypass mod on stock battery." — https://www.factoryminibikes.com/products/bac4000-stage-1-power-upgrade-kit-self-install

Vendor tune chart: "8.5kW 60V Bypass Tune = Use on the Segway X260 or SurRon X 60V with Bypass mod on stock battery." — https://www.factoryminibikes.com/products/bac4000-stage-1-power-upgrade-kit-self-install

Vendor tune chart: "8.5kW 60V Bypass Tune = Use on the Segway X260 or SurRon X 60V with Bypass mod on stock battery." — https://www.factoryminibikes.com/products/bac4000-stage-1-power-upgrade-kit-self-install. The statement is explicitly for the 60V SurRon X, i.e. pre-2026 models only; 2026 LBX is 72V.

Vendor fitment: "Sur Ron LBX / S, Segway X160 / X260, Talaria Sting MX3 / MX4, Talaria XXX, E Ride Pro-S / SS / 2.0..." — https://gritshift.com/products/warp-9-e-bike-rear-sprocket. Years capped at 2025: 2026 LBX is a redesigned platform and the fitment list does not cover it.
Vendor fitment: "Sur Ron LBX / S, Segway X160 / X260..." — https://gritshift.com/products/warp-9-e-bike-rear-sprocket
Vendor fitment: "Sur Ron LBX / S, Segway X160 / X260..." — https://gritshift.com/products/warp-9-e-bike-rear-sprocket
Vendor fitment: "...Talaria Sting MX3 / MX4, Talaria XXX..." — https://gritshift.com/products/warp-9-e-bike-rear-sprocket. 54T needs a 110-link chain, 60T needs 114-link (same page).
Vendor fitment: "...Talaria Sting MX3 / MX4, Talaria XXX..." — https://gritshift.com/products/warp-9-e-bike-rear-sprocket

GritShift sells billet rear sprockets for this platform ("EBikeZilla Billet Rear Sprocket for Sur Ron, Segway, & Talaria E-Bikes" — https://gritshift.com/products/ebikezilla-e-bike-rear-sprocket-sur-ron-segway-talaria), but our catalog SKU is GritShift-branded and the exact SKU-to-listing match is unconfirmed. Fit pattern matches Sur-Ron LBX; confirm SKU.

Same basis as the LBX row: GritShift billet sprocket listings cover Sur Ron/Segway/Talaria (https://gritshift.com/products/ebikezilla-e-bike-rear-sprocket-sur-ron-segway-talaria), but exact catalog-SKU match unconfirmed.
GritShift billet sprocket listings cover Sur Ron/Segway/Talaria (https://gritshift.com/products/ebikezilla-e-bike-rear-sprocket-sur-ron-segway-talaria); exact catalog-SKU match unconfirmed.

54T sprockets in GritShift's line fit "Sur Ron LBX / S, Segway X160 / X260, Talaria Sting MX3 / MX4, Talaria XXX" and "The 54T requires a 110-link chain" (https://gritshift.com/products/warp-9-e-bike-rear-sprocket), but that listing is Warp 9-branded; our GritShift-branded 54T SKU match is unconfirmed.

Same basis: 54T fitment pattern covers Segway X160/X260 per https://gritshift.com/products/warp-9-e-bike-rear-sprocket, but exact GritShift-branded SKU match unconfirmed. 54T requires a 110-link chain.
Same basis: 54T fitment pattern covers Talaria Sting MX3/MX4 per https://gritshift.com/products/warp-9-e-bike-rear-sprocket, but exact GritShift-branded SKU match unconfirmed.

Vendor model list: "Surron Light Bee X, Talaria Sting (MX3), Talaria Sting R (MX4), Talaria XXX", "Fronts and rears available" — https://parts.electriccyclerider.com/products/warp-9-custom-wheels. Years capped at 2025 (2026 LBX redesigned platform not covered).
Vendor model list explicitly names "Talaria Sting R (MX4)" — https://parts.electriccyclerider.com/products/warp-9-custom-wheels
Vendor model list explicitly names "Talaria XXX" — https://parts.electriccyclerider.com/products/warp-9-custom-wheels

Vendor compatible-models list: "Surron LBX", "E Ride Pro S", "E Ride Pro SS 2.0" — https://parts.electriccyclerider.com/products/kke-17-supermoto-wheel-set-for-surron-lbx-e-ride-pro. Years capped at 2025 (2026 LBX redesigned platform not covered).

KKE makes an Ultra Bee-specific 17in supermoto set (eBay listing: "KKE 17\" Supermoto Wheels Rims for Surron Ultra Bee 2023-2025" — https://www.ebay.com/itm/314842574659), but that is a different SKU from this LBX set. Do not sell this SKU for Ultra Bee without confirming.

Vendor: "31.8mm center clamp size (OEM fit for Surron LBX/S, Segway X-160/260, Eride Pro-all models, Talaria Sting- all models)" — https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/warp9-3-rise-handlebar-for-surron-segway-talaria. Years capped at 2025; 2026 LBX cockpit specs unconfirmed.
Vendor: "OEM fit for Surron LBX/S, Segway X-160/260..." — https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/warp9-3-rise-handlebar-for-surron-segway-talaria
Vendor: "OEM fit for Surron LBX/S, Segway X-160/260..." — https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/warp9-3-rise-handlebar-for-surron-segway-talaria
Vendor: "OEM fit for... Talaria Sting- all models" — https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/warp9-3-rise-handlebar-for-surron-segway-talaria

Vendor claim "Talaria Sting- all models" (https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/warp9-3-rise-handlebar-for-surron-segway-talaria) may predate the MX5 Pro; MX5 Pro clamp spec not explicitly confirmed.

The XXX is not a "Sting" and is not named in the vendor statement (https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/warp9-3-rise-handlebar-for-surron-segway-talaria). Cockpit is believed shared 31.8mm but unconfirmed.

Vendor product page: "Sirris F43 Front Fork | Surron Ultra Bee Suspension Upgrade" — the F43 "seamlessly integrates with the Surron Ultra Bee's OEM front-end setup as a direct swap" — https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/sirris-f43-fork-for-surron-ultra-bee
Vendor product page: "Sirris R46 Shock for Surron Ultra Bee" — https://parts.electriccyclerider.com/products/sirris-rear-shock-for-surron-ultra-bee

Manufacturer product page: "FASTACE ALX13RC 2.0 Original Factory Front Fork Suspension For Surron Light Bee, Talaria MX3, Eride Pro S/SS, 79 Bike" — https://fastace.com/products/fastace-alx13rc-2-0-original-factory-front-fork-suspension-for-surron-light-bee-talaria-mx3-eride-pro-s-ss-79-bike. Years capped at 2025 (2026 LBX ships a redesigned platform with upgraded suspension; carryover unconfirmed).

Fastace's own fitment names "Talaria MX3", not MX4 (https://fastace.com/products/fastace-alx13rc-2-0-original-factory-front-fork-suspension-for-surron-light-bee-talaria-mx3-eride-pro-s-ss-79-bike). Vendors sell it under a generic "Surron/Talaria/Segway" banner (https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/fastace-fork-for-surron-talaria-segway). MX4 steerer/axle match unconfirmed.
Community-standard Light Bee rear shock upgrade (frequently paired with the ALX13RC fork), but no explicit manufacturer/vendor fitment statement was captured in this pass. Confirm before selling.

Popular community front-suspension swap for rigid-fork Super73 Z-series bikes; no manufacturer/vendor statement naming the Z Miami was found. Headtube/axle compatibility must be confirmed per model year.

Vendor: "Dunlop Performance Off-Road Tire for Talaria Sting, Sur-Ron LB-X and Segway X260. 70/100-19 is for 19'' Original Rim (OEM)" — https://radmotousa.com/products/off-road-tire-dunlop-geomax-mx53-for-surron-and-talaria. Years capped at 2025 pending confirmation the 2026 platform keeps 19in rims.

Vendor: "...for Talaria Sting, Sur-Ron LB-X and Segway X260. 70/100-19 is for 19'' Original Rim (OEM)" — https://radmotousa.com/products/off-road-tire-dunlop-geomax-mx53-for-surron-and-talaria. MX4 runs OEM 19in rims front and rear.
Vendor: "...for Talaria Sting, Sur-Ron LB-X and Segway X260. 70/100-19 is for 19'' Original Rim (OEM)" — https://radmotousa.com/products/off-road-tire-dunlop-geomax-mx53-for-surron-and-talaria

Vendor statement covers "Talaria Sting" generically (https://radmotousa.com/products/off-road-tire-dunlop-geomax-mx53-for-surron-and-talaria); MX5 Pro rim sizes not explicitly confirmed in that statement.

505 Cheater rear sizes (90/100-16, 110/100-18) do NOT fit the stock 19in rear rim. Charged Cycle Works: "you cannot buy a 'sticky' 18\" or 19\" rear tire that will fit on the Surron" — requires a 16in/18in rear wheel conversion (https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/shinko-r525-hybrid-cheater-tire-90-100x16-for-sur-ron-or-segway-x-260). Only sell alongside a wheel conversion.

Same caveat as the 505 set: the 505 rear sizes need an aftermarket 16in/18in rear wheel; stock LBX rear is 19in (see https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/shinko-r525-hybrid-cheater-tire-90-100x16-for-sur-ron-or-segway-x-260). Front 546 fitment on the stock 19in front also needs size confirmation.

Vendor product page: "Shinko 244 Dual Sport Tires for Sur-Ron or Segway X260" — https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/shinko-244-dual-sport-tires. Years capped at 2025 (2026 platform rims unconfirmed).
Vendor product page: "Shinko 244 Dual Sport Tires for Sur-Ron or Segway X260" — https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/shinko-244-dual-sport-tires
The RCR runs 17in moped rims and the 244 in 2.75-17 is a common community fitment, but no vendor statement naming the RCR was found. Confirm size on the RCR rim before listing.

2.75-17 moped tire (sold as a moped knobby: https://radmotousa.com/products/shinko-2-75-17-knobby-moped-tire-sr241) commonly run on the RCR's 17in rims; the RCR itself is not named by any vendor statement found. Community-reported fitment only.
Community-standard street tire for the RCR's 17in moped wheels; no manufacturer/vendor RCR fitment statement found in this pass. Confirm sizes against RCR rim width before listing.

Manufacturer product page: "ONYX RCR 72V Rear Shocks" — https://onyxmotors.com/products/onyx-rcr-rear-shocks. Title specifies the RCR 72V; confirm shock length (stock 350mm) for older/CTY-adjacent variants.

Charged Cycle Works sells a tall/extra-padded seat for this bike ("eBikeZilla Comfort Seat for Surron Light Bee" — https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/comfort-seat-surron-light-bee), but that listing is eBikeZilla-manufactured; our CCW-branded SKU match is unconfirmed. Fit target is clearly the Light Bee.
Manufacturer fitment: fits "All R-Series Models" — https://super73.com/products/grzly-tire-override-single-5. Note: 20x5 is the REAR size; front runs the 20x4.5.
Manufacturer fitment: fits "All R-Series Models" (R Brooklyn is R-Series) — https://super73.com/products/grzly-tire-override-single-5. 20x5 is the rear size; front is 20x4.5.
Manufacturer fitment: fits "All S-Series Models" — https://super73.com/products/grzly-tire-override-single-5. 20x5 rear / 20x4.5 front.

Manufacturer fitment: "SUPER73-Z Miami (2023 and earlier)" — https://super73.com/products/grzly-tire-override-single-5. Years limited to 2023 and earlier per Super73's own statement; do NOT list for 2024-2025 Z Miami.
Manufacturer fitment: "All R-Series Models, All S-Series Models, SUPER73-Z Miami (2023 and earlier)..." — https://super73.com/products/bdgr-tire-override-single-4-5
Manufacturer fitment: "All R-Series Models..." — https://super73.com/products/bdgr-tire-override-single-4-5
Manufacturer fitment: "All S-Series Models..." — https://super73.com/products/bdgr-tire-override-single-4-5
Manufacturer fitment: "SUPER73-Z Miami (2023 and earlier)" — https://super73.com/products/bdgr-tire-override-single-4-5. Do NOT list for 2024-2025 Z Miami.

Vendor claims it is "designed to fit all Super73 models" but also says "Confirm your Super73 model, wheel size, mounting points, connector type and installation space before ordering" — https://evmod.us/products/low-rise-handlebar-for-super73. Brand-level claim, no per-model statement; verify per model.
Same basis: "designed to fit all Super73 models" with a confirm-before-ordering caveat — https://evmod.us/products/low-rise-handlebar-for-super73
Same basis: "designed to fit all Super73 models" with a confirm-before-ordering caveat — https://evmod.us/products/low-rise-handlebar-for-super73

Same basis: "designed to fit all Super73 models" with a confirm-before-ordering caveat — https://evmod.us/products/low-rise-handlebar-for-super73. Claim predates 2026 models, so 2026 M1D/B1G/MZFT are deliberately excluded.
Vendor: "Meet our 60 volt 'Aura' long-range battery for the Sur-Ron & Segway X160/X260" — https://powerfullithium.com/products/60v-aura-battery-for-sur-ron. Drop-in on stock controller.
Vendor: "...for the Sur-Ron & Segway X160/X260" — https://powerfullithium.com/products/60v-aura-battery-for-sur-ron. Drop-in on stock controller.

Vendor says "for the Sur-Ron" without naming the Light Bee X specifically (https://powerfullithium.com/products/60v-aura-battery-for-sur-ron). 60V architecture matches only the 60V-era Light Bee (Ultra Bee is 74V, Storm Bee higher, 2026 LBX is 72V) — hence the check status and year cap.

Vendor: a "60V 10A charger" for the "Surron Light Bee" with "auto switch from 110V to 220V" — https://alienrides.com/products/surron-light-bee-10a-charger. 67.2V output charges 60V packs ONLY — never the 2026 72V platform, hence the hard year cap.

X260 uses the same 60V/67.2V charge architecture and third-party listings market 67.2V 10A chargers as "Compatible with Surron Light Bee X Segway X260" (https://www.amazon.com/67-2V-10A-Compatible-Electric-Waterproof/dp/B0FCMC84T6), but the Alien Rides page itself does not name Segway. Confirm connector before selling.

Vendor: fits "Talaria Sting MX3/MX4/MX5 perfectly" and "fits under the OEM lid without modifications"; "not compatible with your OEM 60v controller" — pair with X-9000 or Torp TC1000 — https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/72v-42ah-battery-for-talaria-sting

Vendor names "Talaria Sting MX3/MX4/MX5" (https://chargedcycleworks.com/products/72v-42ah-battery-for-talaria-sting); our bike is the MX5 Pro variant, not explicitly named. Confirm Pro-variant tray/lid fit.

GritShift carries footpeg lines for the Sur-Ron/Segway/Talaria platform (see https://gritshift.com/collections/segway-x260-parts-accessories), but no explicit per-model fitment statement for this exact SKU was captured. Confirm SKU fitment.
Same basis: platform-level GritShift coverage, no per-model statement captured for this SKU. Confirm before listing.

Warp 9 makes e-moto footpegs sold across the Sur-Ron/Talaria/E-Ride platform by scene vendors (e.g. https://darwinev.com/product-category/accessories/warp9/), but no explicit per-model fitment statement was captured for this SKU. Confirm.
Same basis: platform-level Warp 9 coverage via scene vendors, no per-model statement captured for this SKU. Confirm.

Ships as the display in some ASI BAC4000 Sur-Ron/Segway kits (emotobros kit includes "EggRider v2 display" — https://emotobros.com/products/ols/products/asi-bac4000-plug-and-play-kit-for-surron). NOT a plug-in on the stock controller — only relevant with an ASI controller conversion.
Same basis: bundled with ASI BAC4000 Sur-Ron/Segway conversion kits (https://emotobros.com/products/ols/products/asi-bac4000-plug-and-play-kit-for-surron); useless on the stock controller.
